bands.headline: You barely dipped
bands.detail: Try a deeper, smoother knee bend before you rise. Most good reps have a noticeable dip, not a straight-leg push.
tips: Start balanced, then dip under control
Avoid collapsing forward—stay tall through your torso

bands.headline: Decent dip, could be more consistent
bands.detail: You're getting some knee bend, but it varies. Aim for a repeatable dip depth each rep.
tips: Dip then rise in one smooth motion

bands.headline: Great knee dip
bands.detail: Good depth and timing. Keep it smooth and repeatable.

bands.headline: Not enough extension at release
bands.detail: Your elbow isn't finishing. Focus on reaching up through the shot and fully extending the shooting arm.

bands.headline: Good, but finish a bit stronger
bands.detail: Your extension is close. Clean up the last part of the motion to fully finish the shot. But if it works for you and you’re consistent, it's not a big deal.

bands.headline: Follow-through is low
bands.detail: Your wrist isn't finishing high enough. Snap the wrist and keep fingers pointing down, holding the follow-through.

bands.headline: Solid follow-through
bands.detail: You're getting a decent finish, but it could be more pronounced and stable.

bands.headline: Great follow-through
bands.detail: Nice wrist snap and finish. Keep it.
tips: Consistency is the goal—repeat this rep

bands.headline: Release looks slow
bands.detail: Try a quicker, more decisive release. Smooth doesn't mean slow—aim for a sharp finish.

bands.headline: Good tempo
bands.detail: Your release speed is okay. Improve consistency by keeping the motion continuous.
tips: One smooth chain: dip > rise > release

bands.headline: Explosive release
bands.detail: Nice pop at release. Good sign of efficient sequencing.
tips: Keep shoulders relaxed to stay accurate
